informationClass.abstract: Specialized Types are fundamental data constructs whose semantics are rigidly defined by the language itself.

envelope.abstract: Specialized Types are fundamental data constructs whose semantics are rigidly defined by the language itself.

languageEvolution.abstract: Specialized Types are fundamental data constructs whose semantics are rigidly defined by the language itself.

enforcementMechanics.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

explanationMechanics.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

designationMechanics.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

declarativeCompliance.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

integralClass.abstract: Integral primitives represent fixed-width integer values with deterministic size, signedness, and binary representation.

decimalClass.abstract: Decimal and floating-point primitives represent numeric values with fractional components.

booleanClass.abstract: Boolean primitives represent binary truth values used for flags, predicates, admissibility checks, and semantic conditions.

dateTimeClass.abstract: Date and time primitives represent temporal facts and durations with explicit semantics and canonical representation.

identifiersClass.abstract: Identifier primitives represent stable, opaque identity values used for correlation, reference, deduplication, and audit traceability.

specialControlClass.abstract: Special/control primitives represent semantic control markers, not domain data.

enumPrimitive.abstract: enum represents a constrained, finite value space used to eliminate magic numbers, magic strings, and implicit semantic branching.

block.abstract: Block types are used to model opaque or structurally fixed or dynamic data payloads whose internal representation is either intentionally hidden...

opaqueBlock.abstract: Opaque block types represent indivisible, immutable binary payloads with explicitly declared or limited with 26-bits size semantics.

structuralBlock.abstract: Structural blocks mainly represent derived types with schema-defined, immutable data compositions built from primitives and block values.

stream.abstract: Stream types represent sequential, potentially unbounded data carriers designed for controlled input/output, transport, and incremental...

streamCarriers.abstract: Stream carriers represent potentially unbounded, sequential data exchange formats intended for transport of large payloads, continuous data, or...

rawString.abstract: RAW String represents a specialized stream type for large or unbounded textual data that must be processed and transported as a stream rather...

rawBinary.abstract: RAW Binary represents large or streaming binary data that cannot be safely or efficiently represented as bounded opaque blocks.

structuralStream.abstract: Structural stream mainly represent derived types with schema-defined, immutable data compositions built from primitives and block values.

any.abstract: any is a special polymorphic primitive or block-materialized data container that guarantees preservation of the physical properties of the...

tuple.abstract: tuple is a flexible data composition construct that combines multiple values into a single structured block.

version.abstract: version is an immutable factual record composed of four ordered components — major, minor, build, and revision — used to express...

reference.abstract: reference is a specialized embedded data type used to form explicit, canonical references to objects beyond the current application or domain boundary.

expTree.abstract: The exptree is a specialized block-materialized data type that encodes permission derivation, validation, and enforcement logic strictly as data.

error.abstract: represent an immutable failure fact suitable for deterministic classification and evidence preservation;

event.abstract: represent an immutable factual occurrence within a business or system context;

typedArray.abstract: A typed array is an array derived type whose element type is explicitly declared and whose dimensionality (rank) is fixed at declaration time.

derivedArray.abstract: A derived array is a named, first-class array type declared at the domain level.

collection.abstract: A Collection is a semantic derived type representing an iterable container abstraction conceptually based on a one-dimensional array (rank = 1).

record.abstract: A record  represents a structured block-materialized, schema-defined derived value composed of named fields.

dictionary.abstract: A dictionary represents a key–value derived container with explicitly typed keys and values, intended for structured associative data.

class.abstract: A Class is a *capable behavioral construct* that represents a concrete, state-bearing execution unit.

binder.abstract: A Binder is a *capable behavioral construct* that defines a deterministic transformation contract: it maps an input value or structure into...

inline.abstract: An Inline construct is a *capable behavioral construct* that is materialized as a behavior surrogate and provides a localized, embedded...

flow.abstract: A Flow is an *orchestration behavioral construct* that defines a transactional, multi-step execution topology.

action.abstract: An Action is an *authority behavioral construct* that declares a business-meaningful intent which may be executed as part of a flow once admitted.

rule.abstract: A Rule is an *authority behavioral construct* that defines deterministic semantic reasoning logic.

interface.abstract: An Interface is an *authority behavioral construct* that defines a declarative contract of capability and responsibility without providing...

definition.abstract: Definition is a first-class semantic construct that *designates meaning and obligations* for a domain concept.

dataInterface.abstract: A Data Interface is a first-class semantic construct that projects defined meaning onto concrete data surfaces.

semanticInterface.abstract: A Semantic Interface is a first-class semantic construct that *projects defined meaning into admissible behavioral interaction*.

target: what delegate means as a semantic construct in ELIA
delegate.abstract: Delegate is a first-class semantic construct that establishes an explicit trust and execution boundary between E.L.I.A.

explanation.abstract: Explanation is a first-class semantic construct responsible for making *meaning explicit, inspectable, and traceable* for both humans and machines.

domain.abstract: The E.L.I.A Language introduces a new paradigm of data processing - no longer persevered processing data and context.

module.abstract: The module directive establishes or references the runtime and packaging scope within an active domain.

using.abstract: The using directive declares capability usage.

import.abstract: The import directive declares semantic dependency on external domains or exported surfaces.

follow.abstract: The follow directive declares a logical or semantic dependency that gates execution or evaluation.

required.abstract: The required directive declares mandatory presence of values or capabilities.

default.abstract: The default directive declares a fallback value or behavior.

emit.abstract: The emit directive declares explicit emission of observable artifacts.

variableDeclaration.abstract: Variable declarations introduce named intermediate values within a block scope.

normativeRuleClass.abstract: A normative rule class is a compiler-recognized category of rules with strict constraints on:

evaluationStages.abstract: Compile / Resolve / Bind time: planning-time evaluation over declarations, versions, capabilities, registry/type-table and static configuration.

semanticNormativeClass.abstract: A Semantic Normative Class is a semantic category of constraint defined in ANNEX E, describing *what kind of constraint a rule represents*...

dataTypeFunctions.abstract: This section defines normative, deterministic functions applicable to E.L.I.A. data types.

semanticModel.abstract: Semantic model in E.L.I.A. is declarative and deterministic (consists of finite set of primitives).

resilienceModel.abstract: Data and behavior serve fundamentally different roles in a resilient system and are therefore subject to different rules, guarantees, and failure models.

designPrinciples.abstract: The E.L.I.A Language introduces a new paradigm of data processing - no longer persevered processing data and context.

typeRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

typeMetadata.abstract: E.L.I.A. associates every data element with a 64‑bit Type Metadata envelope  that enables deterministic parsing, validation, and serialization...
